purpose: Routeable roadmap for improving the SCBE agent bus into a durable, observable, gated, and trainable coordination layer.
research_principles: guardrails and tripwires before side effects
trace spans for agent runs, tool calls, guardrails, and handoffs
queue groups and no-responder feedback for scalable worker movement
lane-specific scoring instead of one global metric

branches.value: Make every bus run produce readiness labels and block ungoverned remote/live dispatch.
Turn tasks into auditable mission packets with expected outputs, rollback, lease, and risk class.
Generate cheap dry-run artifacts and training data without provider calls or file mutation.
Make bus failures diagnosable through traceable route, gate, dispatch, watcher, and handoff spans.
Route by measured outcome, cost, latency, and failure mode instead of static provider strengths only.
Pause before risky side effects and convert denied actions into help-desk fix plans.
Evaluate each lane by the right metric: executable tests, citations, governance gates, or frozen evals.
Export bus traces and gate outcomes into regularized SFT and multiple-choice datasets.
Resume long-running bus work without duplicating side effects or losing state.
Scale worker movement with capability queues, leases, no-responder state, and fair distribution.
